articles.headline: Top Naxal Commander Madvi Hidma Killed in Encounter in Andhra Pradesh
articles.explainer:
• Madvi Hidma, a prominent Naxalite commander and leader of the Communist Party of India (Maoist), was killed in an encounter with security forces in Alluri Sitharama Raju district, Andhra Pradesh.
• The encounter also resulted in the deaths of his wife and four other rebels, marking a significant blow to the Maoist insurgency.
• Hidma, 44, was a key strategist for the CPI (Maoist) and carried a substantial bounty on his head, involved in numerous attacks against security personnel.
• The operation highlights the ongoing efforts by central and state security agencies to counter Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) in critical regions.
• This incident is expected to significantly disrupt the command and control structure of the Maoist outfit in the Andhra-Odisha Border (AOB) and Bastar regions.

Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Madvi Hidma was a prominent commander of the Communist Party of India (Maoist).
He was killed in Alluri Sitharama Raju district, Andhra Pradesh.

mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of eliminating top Naxal leadership in combating Left-Wing Extremism (LWE).
Analyze the current strategy of security forces in tackling LWE, including intelligence gathering and coordinated operations.

articles.imageDescription: Security personnel conducting an anti-Naxal operation in a forested area.
articles.trivia: The 'Red Corridor' refers to the region in central and eastern India that experiences a significant amount of Naxalite-Maoist insurgency.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of several key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, dismissing petitions challenging its various aspects.
• The ruling emphasized the state's legitimate interest in protecting citizens' data while balancing it with the fundamental right to privacy.
• Petitions had raised concerns regarding the broad powers granted to the Data Protection Board and the potential for surveillance.
• The Court clarified the safeguards embedded in the Act, including the independent nature of the Board and the necessity principle for data processing by state entities.
• This landmark judgment provides clarity for businesses and individuals on data handling and privacy norms in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary.
Fundamental Rights.
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act was passed in 2023.
The Supreme Court upheld its constitutional validity.

mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on the Digital Personal Data Protection Act for individual privacy and state surveillance.
Discuss the balance struck by the Act between data protection and ease of doing business in the digital economy.
Critically evaluate the powers and independence of the Data Protection Board as envisioned by the Act and interpreted by the Court.
Compare India's data protection framework with global standards like GDPR, highlighting similarities and differences.
Suggest further reforms or implementation challenges that might arise in the full operationalization of the Act.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal books, symbolizing judicial pronouncement.
articles.trivia: The landmark K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) by the Supreme Court declared the right to privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India and GCC Make Significant Progress on Free Trade Agreement Talks
articles.explainer:
• India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) concluded the latest round of negotiations for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA), reporting significant progress on key contentious issues.
• Both sides expressed optimism about finalizing the agreement by early next year, potentially boosting bilateral trade to over $200 billion annually.
• Discussions focused on market access for goods, services, rules of origin, and investment protection.
• A comprehensive FTA is expected to provide Indian exports, particularly in textiles, engineering goods, and pharmaceuticals, greater access to the GCC markets.
• For the GCC, the agreement offers enhanced access to India's vast consumer market and investment opportunities.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to maintain the repo rate at its current level, opting for a status quo amidst persistent inflationary pressures.
• The decision was largely in line with market expectations, as the MPC aims to bring inflation within its target range while supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global uncertainties and domestic food price dynamics as key factors influencing the decision.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while remaining supportive of growth.
• Experts suggest that future rate actions will be highly dependent on the trajectory of inflation and global commodity prices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.
The MPC has six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting framework in India aims to keep CPI inflation at 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.

articles.trivia: The first official interest rate in India was introduced in 1935 by the RBI, which was then known as the Bank Rate.

articles.headline: ISRO Accelerates Preparations for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focus on Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, with a primary objective of bringing lunar soil and rock samples back to Earth.
• This mission marks a significant step up from previous Chandrayaan missions, demonstrating India's growing capabilities in advanced space exploration.
• The mission architecture involves a Lander, an Ascent Module, an Orbiter, and a Re-entry Module, necessitating complex rendezvous and docking maneuvers in lunar orbit.
• Scientists are finalizing landing sites near the lunar south pole, known for potential water ice deposits, to collect samples of high scientific value.
• The success of Chandrayaan-4 would place India among an elite group of nations capable of lunar sample return missions, boosting its prestige in global space research.

Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-4 aims for a lunar sample return mission.
It would be India's first lunar sample return mission.
The mission potentially involves a Lander, Ascent Module, Orbiter, and Re-entry Module.
Lunar south pole is a prime target for sample collection due to potential water ice.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and technological challenges associated with India's Chandrayaan-4 lunar sample return mission.
Analyze the strategic importance of lunar sample return capabilities for India's space program and its standing in global space exploration.

Examine how missions like Chandrayaan-4 contribute to the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in the space sector.
Compare India's lunar exploration strategy with that of other major spacefaring nations, highlighting areas of convergence and divergence.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of a lunar lander on the moon's surface with an ascent module taking off.
articles.trivia: The first successful lunar sample return mission was Luna 16 by the Soviet Union in 1970.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves 'Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban Plus' for Affordable Housing
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved a new scheme, 'Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban Plus (PMAY-U Plus)', aimed at extending affordable housing benefits to a wider section of the urban poor.
• PMAY-U Plus will focus on innovative financing mechanisms, greater private sector participation, and the use of green building technologies.
• The scheme introduces enhanced subsidies for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) and Lower Income Group (LIG) categories, along with provisions for rental housing.
• It also emphasizes slum rehabilitation in partnership with states and provides for housing for migrant workers in urban areas.
• The initiative seeks to achieve the government's vision of 'Housing for All' by 2027, adapting to evolving urban demographic and economic needs.

articles.headline: India and Brazil Deepen Cooperation on South-South Development Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• India and Brazil held high-level consultations to strengthen their collaboration on South-South cooperation, focusing on sustainable development goals and technology transfer.
• The discussions emphasized joint projects in areas such as renewable energy, digital public infrastructure, and agricultural innovation across developing nations.
• Both countries reaffirmed their commitment to multilateralism and reforming global governance institutions to better represent the interests of the Global South.
• This renewed focus comes ahead of upcoming BRICS and G20 summits, where India and Brazil aim to present a united front on issues pertinent to developing countries.
• The partnership seeks to leverage their respective strengths to provide viable and equitable development models for other nations in Africa, Asia, and Latin America.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power today announced a new comprehensive policy aimed at accelerating the deployment of Electric Vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure across India.
• The policy introduces incentives for private players to set up charging stations, including land availability, subsidies for equipment, and relaxed licensing norms.
• It mandates charging infrastructure in new commercial buildings and encourages retrofitting in existing ones, focusing on highways and urban centers.
• Standardization of charging protocols and interoperability between different charging networks are key features to ensure seamless user experience.
• This move is crucial for achieving India's ambitious EV adoption targets and reducing its carbon footprint in the transportation sector.

articles.headline: Archaeologists Unearth New Habitation Layer at Ancient Harappan Site in Haryana
articles.explainer:
• A team of archaeologists has discovered a previously unknown habitation layer at an ancient Harappan site in Rakhigarhi, Haryana, pushing back the estimated age of the settlement.
• The new findings include artifacts, pottery shards, and structural remains that suggest continuous occupation over a longer period than previously thought.
• This discovery provides crucial insights into the evolution of the Indus Valley Civilization and its regional variations.
• Researchers are employing advanced dating techniques to precisely determine the chronology of this newly found layer.
• The site is expected to yield more information about the daily life, trade networks, and cultural practices of the early Harappans.

prelimsPoints: Rakhigarhi is a prominent Harappan site located in Haryana.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Stronger Gender Budgeting Framework
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee has submitted a report recommending significant enhancements to India's gender budgeting framework to ensure more equitable resource allocation.
• The report highlighted gaps in the implementation of gender-sensitive policies and called for greater accountability from ministries and departments.
• Key recommendations include mandating gender audits for all major government schemes and projects, and specific budgetary allocations for women-centric programs.
• It also suggested capacity building for officials involved in planning and expenditure to integrate a gender perspective effectively.
• The committee emphasized that robust gender budgeting is essential for achieving Sustainable Development Goal 5 (Gender Equality) and inclusive national development.

articles.headline: India Unveils National Quantum Mission 2.0 with Focus on Commercialization
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology (DST) unveiled the National Quantum Mission (NQM) 2.0, an enhanced roadmap focusing on the commercialization and industrial application of quantum technologies.
• Building on the initial phase, NQM 2.0 aims to foster a robust quantum ecosystem through public-private partnerships and increased funding for startups.
• Key areas of focus include quantum computing, quantum communication, quantum sensing, and quantum materials, with specific targets for developing indigenous hardware and software.
• The mission seeks to establish Quantum Technology Hubs across academic and research institutions, creating a skilled workforce.
• This strategic initiative positions India as a significant player in the global quantum race, with potential impacts on defense, finance, and healthcare sectors.

articles.headline: Asia's Largest Single-Location Solar Power Project Inaugurated in Rajasthan
articles.explainer:
• A new 2,500 MW single-location solar power project, touted as Asia's largest, was inaugurated today in Rajasthan, significantly boosting India's renewable energy capacity.
• The project, developed by a consortium of public and private sector entities, utilizes advanced photovoltaic technology and smart grid integration.
• It is expected to generate enough clean electricity to power millions of homes, substantially reducing carbon emissions and reliance on fossil fuels.
• The initiative aligns with India's ambitious targets for renewable energy expansion and its commitment to combating climate change.
• Rajasthan's abundant solar insolation and vast barren lands make it an ideal location for such mega-scale renewable energy projects.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Innovate for Health' Policy to Boost Health-Tech Startups

• The policy provides a framework for incubation, funding, regulatory simplification, and market access for innovative healthcare solutions.
• It aims to leverage technology like AI, IoT, and telemedicine to address healthcare delivery challenges, especially in remote and underserved areas.
• Collaboration between public hospitals, research institutions, and private startups is a cornerstone of the policy, encouraging 'Made in India' health solutions.
• This move is expected to revolutionize healthcare access and quality, aligning with the objectives of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.

articles.headline: Indian Army Kicks Off Major Modernization Drive with Focus on Indigenous Systems

• The move is aimed at enhancing operational capabilities while boosting the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) initiative in defense manufacturing.
• Key areas of focus include advanced artillery, drones, anti-tank guided missiles, and communication systems, with significant orders placed with Indian defense PSUs and private firms.
• The Army Chief emphasized the need for future-ready forces capable of operating in diverse terrains and hybrid warfare scenarios.
• This drive is expected to reduce reliance on foreign imports and foster a robust domestic defense industrial base.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Comprehensive Reforms in UN Peacekeeping Operations

articles.headline: India's Digital Payments Ecosystem Continues Robust Growth, New Report Shows

articles.headline: India Unveils National Policy on Critical Minerals to Secure Supply Chains
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines today announced a new National Policy on Critical Minerals, aiming to secure reliable supply chains for minerals essential for advanced technologies and energy transition.
• The policy identifies a list of strategic minerals, including lithium, cobalt, nickel, and rare earth elements, crucial for EV batteries, solar panels, and defense applications.
• It proposes incentives for domestic exploration and mining, international collaborations for resource access, and recycling initiatives.
• The objective is to reduce import dependence and build self-sufficiency in critical minerals, which are vital for India's economic growth and national security.
• This strategic move is expected to attract significant investment in the mining and processing sectors and foster a circular economy approach.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Amphibian Species in Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian herpetologists has announced the discovery of a new species of frog in the highly biodiverse Western Ghats region, highlighting its ecological significance.
• The newly identified amphibian, characterized by unique genetic markers and morphological features, adds to the rich endemic fauna of the region.
• This discovery underscores the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts in the Western Ghats, which faces threats from deforestation and climate change.
• Researchers used a combination of fieldwork, genetic analysis, and bioacoustics to confirm the new species.
• The finding contributes valuable data to understanding amphibian evolution and distribution patterns in biodiversity hotspots.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Climate Change.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Proposes National AI Ethics Framework for Responsible AI Development
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has released a consultation paper proposing a comprehensive National AI Ethics Framework to guide responsible development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence in India.
• The framework emphasizes principles such as fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, and privacy in AI systems.
• It aims to address potential risks like algorithmic bias, job displacement, and misuse of AI, while harnessing its benefits for economic growth and social good.
• The paper invites feedback from stakeholders, including industry, academia, civil society, and the public, to refine the guidelines.
• This proactive step positions India as a leader in establishing ethical guardrails for AI, ensuring human-centric and inclusive AI innovation.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tourism Launches 'Gramin Paryatan Protsahan Yojana' for Rural Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism today launched the 'Gramin Paryatan Protsahan Yojana' (Rural Tourism Promotion Scheme) to develop and promote rural tourism circuits across India.
• The scheme aims to empower local communities, preserve cultural heritage, and create sustainable livelihoods in rural areas through tourism.
• It will provide financial assistance for infrastructure development, skill training for local guides, and digital marketing of rural destinations.
• Emphasis is placed on showcasing unique local arts, crafts, cuisine, and traditional practices to attract both domestic and international tourists.
• This initiative is expected to diversify India's tourism offerings and ensure equitable distribution of tourism benefits to grassroots levels.

articles.headline: Government Launches National Program on Climate-Resilient Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare today launched a comprehensive 'National Program on Climate-Resilient Agriculture' to equip farmers with adaptive strategies against climate change.
• The program focuses on promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and climate-smart farming practices.
• It integrates weather advisories, soil health management, and livestock management to build resilience across agricultural value chains.
• Pilot projects will be initiated in vulnerable districts, with knowledge dissemination through Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs) and digital platforms.
• This initiative is crucial for ensuring food security and sustaining agricultural productivity in the face of increasing climatic variability.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent killing of Naxalite commander Madvi Hidma, consider the following statements:
1. Madvi Hidma was a prominent leader of the Communist Party of India (Maoist).
2. The encounter took place in the Bastar region of Chhattisgarh.
3. His elimination is considered a major setback for Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) operations in the region.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Madvi Hidma was indeed a prominent commander of the Communist Party of India (Maoist). Statement 2 is incorrect: The encounter took place in Alluri Sitharama Raju district, Andhra Pradesh, not the Bastar region of Chhattisgarh. Statement 3 is correct: The killing of a top commander like Hidma is considered a significant blow to LWE operations.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for determining the policy interest rates to achieve the inflation target in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body constituted by the Central Government under Section 45ZB of the amended RBI Act, 1934. It is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark policy interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023:
1. It aims to protect digital personal data by establishing a Data Protection Board of India.
2. The Supreme Court of India recently struck down several key provisions of the Act as unconstitutional.
3. The Act does not apply to personal data processed outside the territory of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Act establishes the Data Protection Board of India to enforce its provisions. Statement 2 is incorrect: The Supreme Court upheld the constitutional validity of several key provisions, it did not strike them down. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Act has extraterritorial application, applying to processing of personal data outside India if it is in connection with offering goods or services to Data Principals in India or profiling such Data Principals.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about South-South Cooperation is/are correct?
1. It refers to the exchange of resources, technology, and knowledge between developing countries.
2. India and Brazil are prominent proponents of South-South Cooperation.
3. The IBSA Dialogue Forum is an example of a trilateral grouping focused on South-South Cooperation.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. South-South Cooperation is a broad framework for collaboration among countries of the Global South. India and Brazil are active participants and advocates for this cooperation. The IBSA Dialogue Forum, comprising India, Brazil, and South Africa, is a well-known example of South-South cooperation.

prelimsMocks.explanation: Rakhigarhi, located in Haryana, is one of the largest and most significant sites of the Indus Valley Civilization (also known as the Harappan Civilization).

GS1.headline: Archaeologists Unearth New Habitation Layer at Ancient Harappan Site in Haryana
GS1.explainer:
• A team of archaeologists has discovered a previously unknown habitation layer at an ancient Harappan site in Rakhigarhi, Haryana, pushing back the estimated age of the settlement.
• The new findings include artifacts, pottery shards, and structural remains that suggest continuous occupation over a longer period than previously thought.
• This discovery provides crucial insights into the evolution of the Indus Valley Civilization and its regional variations.
• Researchers are employing advanced dating techniques to precisely determine the chronology of this newly found layer.
• The site is expected to yield more information about the daily life, trade networks, and cultural practices of the early Harappans.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of several key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, dismissing petitions challenging its various aspects.
• The ruling emphasized the state's legitimate interest in protecting citizens' data while balancing it with the fundamental right to privacy.
• Petitions had raised concerns regarding the broad powers granted to the Data Protection Board and the potential for surveillance.
• The Court clarified the safeguards embedded in the Act, including the independent nature of the Board and the necessity principle for data processing by state entities.
• This landmark judgment provides clarity for businesses and individuals on data handling and privacy norms in India.

GS2.headline: India and GCC Make Significant Progress on Free Trade Agreement Talks
GS2.explainer:
• India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) concluded the latest round of negotiations for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA), reporting significant progress on key contentious issues.
• Both sides expressed optimism about finalizing the agreement by early next year, potentially boosting bilateral trade to over $200 billion annually.
• Discussions focused on market access for goods, services, rules of origin, and investment protection.
• A comprehensive FTA is expected to provide Indian exports, particularly in textiles, engineering goods, and pharmaceuticals, greater access to the GCC markets.
• For the GCC, the agreement offers enhanced access to India's vast consumer market and investment opportunities.

GS2.headline: Union Cabinet Approves 'Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban Plus' for Affordable Housing
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved a new scheme, 'Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban Plus (PMAY-U Plus)', aimed at extending affordable housing benefits to a wider section of the urban poor.
• PMAY-U Plus will focus on innovative financing mechanisms, greater private sector participation, and the use of green building technologies.
• The scheme introduces enhanced subsidies for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) and Lower Income Group (LIG) categories, along with provisions for rental housing.
• It also emphasizes slum rehabilitation in partnership with states and provides for housing for migrant workers in urban areas.
• The initiative seeks to achieve the government's vision of 'Housing for All' by 2027, adapting to evolving urban demographic and economic needs.

GS2.headline: India and Brazil Deepen Cooperation on South-South Development Initiatives

• The discussions emphasized joint projects in areas such as renewable energy, digital public infrastructure, and agricultural innovation across developing nations.
• Both countries reaffirmed their commitment to multilateralism and reforming global governance institutions to better represent the interests of the Global South.
• This renewed focus comes ahead of upcoming BRICS and G20 summits, where India and Brazil aim to present a united front on issues pertinent to developing countries.
• The partnership seeks to leverage their respective strengths to provide viable and equitable development models for other nations in Africa, Asia, and Latin America.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Stronger Gender Budgeting Framework
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee has submitted a report recommending significant enhancements to India's gender budgeting framework to ensure more equitable resource allocation.
• The report highlighted gaps in the implementation of gender-sensitive policies and called for greater accountability from ministries and departments.
• Key recommendations include mandating gender audits for all major government schemes and projects, and specific budgetary allocations for women-centric programs.
• It also suggested capacity building for officials involved in planning and expenditure to integrate a gender perspective effectively.
• The committee emphasized that robust gender budgeting is essential for achieving Sustainable Development Goal 5 (Gender Equality) and inclusive national development.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Comprehensive Reforms in UN Peacekeeping Operations
GS2.explainer:
• India, a leading troop-contributing country, reiterated its call for comprehensive reforms in United Nations Peacekeeping Operations at a high-level UN meeting.
• India emphasized the need for greater consultation with troop-contributing countries (TCCs) in mandate formulation and decision-making processes.
• Concerns were raised regarding the safety and security of peacekeepers, particularly in challenging conflict zones, and the need for better resources and technology.
• India also highlighted the importance of clear, realistic mandates and a robust accountability framework for peacekeeping missions.
• The call for reforms aims to enhance the effectiveness and legitimacy of UN peacekeeping in addressing global conflicts and maintaining international peace and security.

GS3.headline: Top Naxal Commander Madvi Hidma Killed in Encounter in Andhra Pradesh
GS3.explainer:
• Madvi Hidma, a prominent Naxalite commander and leader of the Communist Party of India (Maoist), was killed in an encounter with security forces in Alluri Sitharama Raju district, Andhra Pradesh.
• The encounter also resulted in the deaths of his wife and four other rebels, marking a significant blow to the Maoist insurgency.
• Hidma, 44, was a key strategist for the CPI (Maoist) and carried a substantial bounty on his head, involved in numerous attacks against security personnel.
• The operation highlights the ongoing efforts by central and state security agencies to counter Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) in critical regions.
• This incident is expected to significantly disrupt the command and control structure of the Maoist outfit in the Andhra-Odisha Border (AOB) and Bastar regions.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to maintain the repo rate at its current level, opting for a status quo amidst persistent inflationary pressures.
• The decision was largely in line with market expectations, as the MPC aims to bring inflation within its target range while supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global uncertainties and domestic food price dynamics as key factors influencing the decision.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while remaining supportive of growth.
• Experts suggest that future rate actions will be highly dependent on the trajectory of inflation and global commodity prices.

Inflation targeting framework in India aims to keep CPI inflation at 4% with a band of +/- 2%.

GS3.headline: ISRO Accelerates Preparations for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focus on Lunar Sample Return
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, with a primary objective of bringing lunar soil and rock samples back to Earth.
• This mission marks a significant step up from previous Chandrayaan missions, demonstrating India's growing capabilities in advanced space exploration.
• The mission architecture involves a Lander, an Ascent Module, an Orbiter, and a Re-entry Module, necessitating complex rendezvous and docking maneuvers in lunar orbit.
• Scientists are finalizing landing sites near the lunar south pole, known for potential water ice deposits, to collect samples of high scientific value.
• The success of Chandrayaan-4 would place India among an elite group of nations capable of lunar sample return missions, boosting its prestige in global space research.

GS3.trivia: The first successful lunar sample return mission was Luna 16 by the Soviet Union in 1970.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power today announced a new comprehensive policy aimed at accelerating the deployment of Electric Vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure across India.
• The policy introduces incentives for private players to set up charging stations, including land availability, subsidies for equipment, and relaxed licensing norms.
• It mandates charging infrastructure in new commercial buildings and encourages retrofitting in existing ones, focusing on highways and urban centers.
• Standardization of charging protocols and interoperability between different charging networks are key features to ensure seamless user experience.
• This move is crucial for achieving India's ambitious EV adoption targets and reducing its carbon footprint in the transportation sector.

GS3.headline: India Unveils National Quantum Mission 2.0 with Focus on Commercialization
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology (DST) unveiled the National Quantum Mission (NQM) 2.0, an enhanced roadmap focusing on the commercialization and industrial application of quantum technologies.
• Building on the initial phase, NQM 2.0 aims to foster a robust quantum ecosystem through public-private partnerships and increased funding for startups.
• Key areas of focus include quantum computing, quantum communication, quantum sensing, and quantum materials, with specific targets for developing indigenous hardware and software.
• The mission seeks to establish Quantum Technology Hubs across academic and research institutions, creating a skilled workforce.
• This strategic initiative positions India as a significant player in the global quantum race, with potential impacts on defense, finance, and healthcare sectors.

GS3.headline: Asia's Largest Single-Location Solar Power Project Inaugurated in Rajasthan
GS3.explainer:
• A new 2,500 MW single-location solar power project, touted as Asia's largest, was inaugurated today in Rajasthan, significantly boosting India's renewable energy capacity.
• The project, developed by a consortium of public and private sector entities, utilizes advanced photovoltaic technology and smart grid integration.
• It is expected to generate enough clean electricity to power millions of homes, substantially reducing carbon emissions and reliance on fossil fuels.
• The initiative aligns with India's ambitious targets for renewable energy expansion and its commitment to combating climate change.
• Rajasthan's abundant solar insolation and vast barren lands make it an ideal location for such mega-scale renewable energy projects.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'Innovate for Health' Policy to Boost Health-Tech Startups
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare today unveiled the 'Innovate for Health' policy, a new initiative to foster and support health-tech startups in India.
• The policy provides a framework for incubation, funding, regulatory simplification, and market access for innovative healthcare solutions.
• It aims to leverage technology like AI, IoT, and telemedicine to address healthcare delivery challenges, especially in remote and underserved areas.
• Collaboration between public hospitals, research institutions, and private startups is a cornerstone of the policy, encouraging 'Made in India' health solutions.
• This move is expected to revolutionize healthcare access and quality, aligning with the objectives of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.

GS3.headline: Indian Army Kicks Off Major Modernization Drive with Focus on Indigenous Systems
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Army today initiated a comprehensive modernization drive, prioritizing the induction of indigenously developed weapon systems and platforms.
• The move is aimed at enhancing operational capabilities while boosting the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) initiative in defense manufacturing.
• Key areas of focus include advanced artillery, drones, anti-tank guided missiles, and communication systems, with significant orders placed with Indian defense PSUs and private firms.
• The Army Chief emphasized the need for future-ready forces capable of operating in diverse terrains and hybrid warfare scenarios.
• This drive is expected to reduce reliance on foreign imports and foster a robust domestic defense industrial base.

GS3.headline: India's Digital Payments Ecosystem Continues Robust Growth, New Report Shows
GS3.explainer:
• A new report released by a leading financial technology firm indicates robust and sustained growth in India's digital payments ecosystem, setting new transaction volume records.
• The report highlights significant adoption of UPI (Unified Payments Interface) in both urban and rural areas, driven by ease of use and merchant acceptance.
• Government initiatives like Jan Dhan-Aadhaar-Mobile (JAM) trinity and increasing internet penetration are cited as key enablers.
• The growth is observed across various segments, including peer-to-peer, peer-to-merchant, and bill payments, fostering financial inclusion.
• This trajectory positions India as a global leader in real-time digital transactions, with potential for further innovation in fintech.

GS3.headline: India Unveils National Policy on Critical Minerals to Secure Supply Chains
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines today announced a new National Policy on Critical Minerals, aiming to secure reliable supply chains for minerals essential for advanced technologies and energy transition.
• The policy identifies a list of strategic minerals, including lithium, cobalt, nickel, and rare earth elements, crucial for EV batteries, solar panels, and defense applications.
• It proposes incentives for domestic exploration and mining, international collaborations for resource access, and recycling initiatives.
• The objective is to reduce import dependence and build self-sufficiency in critical minerals, which are vital for India's economic growth and national security.
• This strategic move is expected to attract significant investment in the mining and processing sectors and foster a circular economy approach.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover New Amphibian Species in Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian herpetologists has announced the discovery of a new species of frog in the highly biodiverse Western Ghats region, highlighting its ecological significance.
• The newly identified amphibian, characterized by unique genetic markers and morphological features, adds to the rich endemic fauna of the region.
• This discovery underscores the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts in the Western Ghats, which faces threats from deforestation and climate change.
• Researchers used a combination of fieldwork, genetic analysis, and bioacoustics to confirm the new species.
• The finding contributes valuable data to understanding amphibian evolution and distribution patterns in biodiversity hotspots.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Proposes National AI Ethics Framework for Responsible AI Development
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has released a consultation paper proposing a comprehensive National AI Ethics Framework to guide responsible development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence in India.
• The framework emphasizes principles such as fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, and privacy in AI systems.
• It aims to address potential risks like algorithmic bias, job displacement, and misuse of AI, while harnessing its benefits for economic growth and social good.
• The paper invites feedback from stakeholders, including industry, academia, civil society, and the public, to refine the guidelines.
• This proactive step positions India as a leader in establishing ethical guardrails for AI, ensuring human-centric and inclusive AI innovation.

GS3.headline: Government Launches National Program on Climate-Resilient Agriculture
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare today launched a comprehensive 'National Program on Climate-Resilient Agriculture' to equip farmers with adaptive strategies against climate change.
• The program focuses on promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and climate-smart farming practices.
• It integrates weather advisories, soil health management, and livestock management to build resilience across agricultural value chains.
• Pilot projects will be initiated in vulnerable districts, with knowledge dissemination through Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs) and digital platforms.
• This initiative is crucial for ensuring food security and sustaining agricultural productivity in the face of increasing climatic variability.
